elements.description: One row per customer we sell to (account, company or person); used to describe who we sell to and to slice sales and related measures by customer attributes such as name, segment or region. Keywords: client, account, buyer.

children.description: Customer name we sell to on this row. Use to filter and group sales by customer. Free-text name, e.g. 'Acme Corp'; Partial-text match. Keywords: client, account.
Customer segment label. Use to compare sales and customers by segment. Free-text category, e.g. 'Enterprise', 'SMB'; Partial-text match.
Customer region where the customer is based. Use to analyze customers and sales by region. Free-text region, e.g. 'EMEA', 'North America'; Partial-text match.
Customer country. Use to analyze customers and sales by country. Free-text country, e.g. 'United States', 'Germany'; Partial-text match.

elements.description: One row per manufacturing plant or site; used to identify where we run production, hold inventory and place purchase orders so operational measures can be analyzed by plant. Keywords: factory, site, facility.

children.description: Plant name we operate. Use to slice production, inventory and purchasing by plant. Free-text name, e.g. 'Plant A', 'Berlin Plant'; Partial-text match. Keywords: factory, site, facility.
Plant region where we operate. Use to group plant-related metrics by region. Free-text region, e.g. 'EMEA', 'APAC'; Partial-text match. Not a customer region field.
Plant country. Use to report production, inventory or purchasing by country. Free-text country, e.g. 'France', 'Brazil'; Partial-text match.
Plant city. Use to analyze plant activity by city location. Free-text city, e.g. 'Chicago', 'Munich'; Partial-text match.
Year attribute for the plant as stored in data. Use to slice plant-level analysis by year. Whole-number year, e.g. 2020, 2024.

elements.description: One row per vendor or supplier we buy from; used to describe who we purchase from and to analyze purchase orders and inbound supply by vendor attributes. Keywords: supplier, vendor base.

children.description: Vendor name we buy from. Use to filter and group purchase orders by vendor. Free-text name, e.g. 'Global Metals'; Partial-text match. Keywords: supplier.
Vendor type classification. Use to compare purchasing patterns by vendor type. Free-text category, e.g. 'Raw Material', 'Logistics'; Partial-text match.
Vendor country. Use to analyze purchasing and supply by vendor country. Free-text country, e.g. 'China', 'USA'; Partial-text match.
Vendor rating score. Use to rank or filter vendors by performance. Numeric score, e.g. 3.5, 4.0.

elements.description: One row per physical asset or device we run in our operations; used to describe machines and equipment and to relate production logs or line assignments back to specific assets. Keywords: machine, equipment.

children.description: Asset or machine name we run. Use to analyze production and downtime by machine. Free-text name, e.g. 'Press 1'; Partial-text match. Keywords: machine, equipment.
Asset type or class. Use to compare performance and downtime by equipment type. Free-text category, e.g. 'Press', 'Conveyor'; Partial-text match.
Asset manufacturer. Use to analyze assets and performance by manufacturer. Free-text name, e.g. 'OEM Co.'; Partial-text match.
Location of the asset within the plant. Use to compare equipment by area. Free-text location, e.g. 'Line 1', 'Bay 3'; Partial-text match.

elements.description: One row per production line within a plant; used to describe how equipment is organized in the factory and to analyze production logs, throughput and downtime by line. Keywords: work center, line, cell.

children.description: Production line name we run. Use to analyze production and downtime by line. Free-text name, e.g. 'Line 1', 'Filling Line'; Partial-text match. Keywords: work center, cell.
Location within the plant for this line. Use to compare lines by area. Free-text code or label, e.g. 'A1', 'Zone 3'; Partial-text match.
Line type classification. Use to analyze performance by line type. Free-text category, e.g. 'Assembly', 'Packaging'; Partial-text match.
Asset name associated with this line. Use when you need asset text on line records. Free-text name, e.g. 'Press 1'; Partial-text match. Not a product name.
Manufacturer of the asset on this line. Use to view line performance by equipment maker. Free-text name, e.g. 'OEM Co.'; Partial-text match.

elements.description: One row per snapshot of inventory for a given product, plant and date; used to analyze on-hand quantities and inventory value over time by product, location and calendar. Keywords: stock, on hand.

children.description: Inventory snapshot date for this row. Use to analyze inventory over time. Calendar date, e.g. 2024-03-31.
Product name in inventory. Use to view on-hand and related metrics by product. Free-text name, e.g. 'Widget A'; Partial-text match. Not a customer or vendor.
Plant name holding this inventory. Use to analyze inventory by plant. Free-text plant name, e.g. 'Plant A'; Partial-text match.
On-hand inventory quantity for the product and plant on this date. Use to see stock levels. Whole-number quantity, e.g. 10, 250.
Reorder threshold quantity for this product and plant. Use to flag items at or below reorder. Whole-number quantity, e.g. 50, 100.
Quantity of this product in transit to the plant. Use to see pipeline inventory. Whole-number quantity, e.g. 5, 40.

elements.description: One row per purchase order record (typically order or order line) for a vendor, plant and date; used to analyze what we buy, when we buy it and from whom, including quantities and costs. Keywords: PO, supplier order.

children.description: Purchase order number for this row. Use to filter or group purchase activity by PO. Code or numeric ID, e.g. 4500123.
Purchase order date for this record. Use to analyze purchasing over time. Calendar date, e.g. 2024-03-31.
Vendor name we buy from on this order. Use to analyze purchasing by vendor. Free-text name, e.g. 'Global Metals'; Partial-text match. Not a customer name.
Plant receiving this purchase order. Use to analyze purchasing by plant. Free-text plant name, e.g. 'Plant A'; Partial-text match.
Ordered quantity on this purchase order row. Use to see purchased units. Whole-number quantity, e.g. 100, 250.
Unit cost for the purchased item on this row. Use to analyze purchase prices. Numeric amount, e.g. 2.50, 15.75.
Total purchase amount for this row (quantity × unit cost as stored). Use to analyze spend by PO line. Numeric amount, e.g. 250, 1575.50.
Promised lead time for this order in days. Use to compare promised vs actual delivery speed. Whole-number days, e.g. 5, 10.
Actual delivery time for this order in days. Use to analyze real lead times. Whole-number days, e.g. 6, 12.
On-time delivery flag for this order. Use to track delivery reliability. TRUE/FALSE value, e.g. TRUE for on-time.

elements.description: One row per production log entry for a specific product, production line, date and device; used to analyze what we produced, how much and on which equipment and line over time. Keywords: production events, machine log, shop floor log.

children.description: Production log date for this entry. Use to analyze production by day. Calendar date, e.g. 2024-03-31.
Date and time of the production log entry. Use for intraday sequencing and timing. Date-time value, e.g. 2024-03-31 14:30.
Year for this production log entry. Use to group production metrics by year. Whole-number year, e.g. 2023, 2024.
Production line name for this log entry. Use to analyze production and downtime by line. Free-text name, e.g. 'Line 1'; Partial-text match.
Product name produced in this log entry. Use to analyze output and scrap by product. Free-text name, e.g. 'Widget A'; Partial-text match. Not an asset name.
Shift label for this log entry. Use to compare production and downtime by shift. Free-text label, e.g. 'Day', 'Night'; Partial-text match.
Planned production quantity for this log period. Use to compare planned vs actual output. Whole-number quantity, e.g. 1000, 500.
Actual produced quantity in this log period. Use to measure total output. Whole-number quantity, e.g. 900, 480.
Good units produced in this log period. Use to measure good output. Whole-number quantity, e.g. 880, 470. Keywords: good output, right-first-time units.
Scrap units produced in this log period. Use to analyze waste and yield. Whole-number quantity, e.g. 20, 10. Keywords: defects, reject units.
Runtime duration for this log period. Use to analyze machine running time. Numeric duration value, e.g. 3600, 7200. Keywords: uptime, run time.
Downtime duration for this log period. Use to analyze machine downtime. Numeric duration value, e.g. 600, 1800. Keywords: outages, stop time.

elements.description: One row per sales transaction or order line for a product, customer and date; used to analyze what we sell, who we sell to and sales amounts and quantities over time. Keywords: revenue, customer orders.

children.description: Sales order number for this row. Use to filter or group sales activity by order. Code or numeric ID, e.g. 100234.
Sales transaction or order line date. Use to analyze sales over time. Calendar date, e.g. 2024-03-31.
Year for this sales row. Use to group or filter sales by year. Whole-number year, e.g. 2023, 2024.
Customer name we sell to on this sales row. Use to analyze sales by customer. Free-text name, e.g. 'Acme Corp'; Partial-text match. Not a vendor name.
Sales region for this row. Use to analyze sales by region. Free-text region, e.g. 'EMEA', 'APAC'; Partial-text match.
Product name or code on this sales row. Use to analyze sales by product. Free-text value, e.g. 'Widget A'; Partial-text match. Not a customer name.
Product category on this sales row. Use to compare sales across categories. Free-text category, e.g. 'Hardware', 'Service'; Partial-text match.
Quantity sold on this sales row. Use to measure units sold. Whole-number quantity, e.g. 5, 20.
Unit price for this sales row. Use to analyze price levels and revenue. Numeric amount, e.g. 19.99, 250.
Discount value applied on this sales row. Use to analyze discounts and net prices. Numeric amount, e.g. 5, 12.5.
Sales amount for this row as stored (typically net revenue). Use to analyze revenue. Numeric amount, e.g. 99.95, 500.
Cost amount associated with this sales row. Use to analyze cost of goods sold. Numeric amount, e.g. 60, 300.
Profit for this sales row (revenue minus cost as stored). Use to analyze margin. Numeric amount, e.g. 39.95, 200.

elements.description: One row per summarized sales result for a given customer and date (for example, daily totals); used to analyze sales at an aggregated level without querying individual transactions. Keywords: aggregated sales, sales summary.

children.description: Date for this summarized sales row. Use to analyze aggregated sales by day. Calendar date, e.g. 2024-03-31.
Customer name for this summarized sales row. Use to analyze aggregated sales by customer. Free-text name, e.g. 'Acme Corp'; Partial-text match.
Summarized sales amount for this customer and date. Use to analyze revenue at an aggregated level. Numeric amount, e.g. 1000, 2500.

elements.description: One row per calendar date covering the range of the operations data; used as the shared calendar for filtering all fact tables and for time intelligence such as year-to-date and period comparisons. Keywords: calendar, working day.

children.description: Calendar date. Use as the main date filter for all fact tables and time intelligence. Date value, e.g. 2024-03-31.
Calendar year for this date. Use to group or filter metrics by year. Whole-number year, e.g. 2023, 2024.
Calendar quarter label for this date. Use to group results by quarter. Text label, e.g. 'Q1', 'Q3'; Partial-text match.
Calendar month name for this date. Use to group metrics by month label. Text label, e.g. 'January', 'Oct'; Partial-text match.
Year-month label for this date. Use to group results by month in order. Text label, e.g. '2024-01'; Partial-text match.
Week-of-year number for this date. Use to group metrics by calendar week. Whole-number week, e.g. 5, 42.
Day-of-week name for this date. Use to group results by weekday. Text label, e.g. 'Monday', 'Fri'; Partial-text match.
Weekend flag for this date. Use to separate weekend vs weekday performance. TRUE/FALSE value, e.g. TRUE for weekend.

elements.description: One row per product we sell or stock; used to describe what we sell or purchase and to analyze sales, inventory and production by product attributes such as name, category or family. Keywords: SKU, item, part.

children.description: Product code we use to identify the item. Use when working with product identifiers. Text code, e.g. 'P-1001'; Partial-text match.
Product name we sell or stock. Use to analyze sales, inventory and production by product. Free-text name, e.g. 'Widget A'; Partial-text match. Keywords: SKU, item, part.
Product category. Use to compare metrics across product categories. Free-text category, e.g. 'Hardware', 'Service'; Partial-text match.
Product subcategory. Use to drill into product category detail. Free-text label, e.g. 'Small Widgets'; Partial-text match.
Standard or list price for the product as stored. Use to compare price levels. Numeric amount, e.g. 19.99, 250.
Standard cost for the product as stored. Use to analyze cost and margin. Numeric amount, e.g. 10, 150.

elements.description: This is a measures-only table with no row-level grain; used as a curated container for business KPIs and reusable calculations that are referenced from reports and other queries. Keywords: KPIs, metrics, performance indicators.

children.description: Total sales revenue from Sales[Amount]. Keywords: revenue, turnover, sales.
Total gross profit from Sales[Profit]. Keywords: margin, earnings, gross profit.
Gross profit as a percentage of sales revenue. Keywords: gross margin %, profitability.
Total sales quantity. Keywords: units sold, volume.
Average realized selling price per sold unit.
Year-to-date sales based on the model Date table. Keywords: YTD revenue, year-to-date sales.
Year-to-date profit based on the model Date table. Keywords: YTD margin, year-to-date profit.
Total on-hand inventory quantity. Keywords: stock level, on hand quantity.
Total inventory quantity currently in transit. Keywords: pipeline stock, in-transit inventory.
Shortfall quantity where inventory is below reorder threshold. Keywords: stockout risk, at-risk items.
Count of product-plant-date combinations where quantity is below reorder level. Keywords: risky SKUs, stockout SKU count.
Total purchase order spend. Keywords: PO spend, purchasing cost.
Count of purchase order rows.
Count of purchase order rows delivered on time.
Percentage of purchase order rows delivered on time. Keywords: on-time delivery, OTIF, supplier reliability.
Average actual days minus promised days across purchase orders.
Total produced quantity. Keywords: output, throughput.
Total good units produced. Keywords: good output, first-pass yield units.
Total scrapped units. Keywords: defect units, waste.
Good units as a percentage of total production quantity. Keywords: yield %, first-pass yield, FPY.
Scrap units as a percentage of total production quantity. Keywords: defect rate %, scrap ratio.
Total production runtime minutes. Keywords: uptime, run time, operating time.
Total production downtime minutes. Keywords: downtime, outages, stop time.
Downtime minutes divided by runtime plus downtime minutes. Keywords: downtime rate %, outage rate.
Actual production quantity divided by planned production quantity. Keywords: plan adherence %, schedule compliance.
GOVERNED. Availability = runtime minutes / (runtime minutes + downtime minutes). Use as the official availability KPI. Keywords: uptime %, machine availability.
GOVERNED. Performance = produced quantity / planned quantity. Use as the official performance KPI. Keywords: speed %, rate efficiency, throughput performance.
GOVERNED. Quality = good units / produced quantity. Use as the official quality KPI. Keywords: right-first-time %, defect-free rate.
GOVERNED. Overall Equipment Effectiveness = Availability × Performance × Quality. Use as the official OEE KPI. Keywords: OEE, equipment effectiveness, manufacturing efficiency.
GOVERNED. Production quantity for the same period last year based on the model Date table. Keywords: prior-year output, last-year production.
GOVERNED. Year-over-year % change in production quantity vs the same period last year. Keywords: YoY growth %, production growth rate.
GOVERNED. Year-to-date production quantity based on the model Date table. Keywords: YTD output, year-to-date production.
GOVERNED. Good units produced for the same period last year based on the model Date table. Keywords: prior-year good output, last-year good units.
GOVERNED. Year-over-year % change in good units vs the same period last year. Keywords: YoY quality %, good units growth rate.
